ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-08-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared via zoom for David Coffing (administrator).
Thomas Cain appeared via zoom for American Research Bureau.

Discussion held in regard to the time spent looking for heirs, that there were 50 to 70 heirs on this case, and that Mr. Vaught's client traveled to California on two separate occasions to assist on the matter. Final report and account of administrator etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bernardo. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Cain, via Zoom, for American Research Bureau, Assignee.

The court notes that Mr. Vaught is not present at today's hearing. Mr. Cain reports that he believed that Mr. Vaught would be present today.

After additional discussion, an Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Jon Vaught to appear on Nov-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Continued To Aug-03-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught, specially appearing for David Coffing, Administrator; Thomas Cain, via Zoom, for American Research Bureau, Assignee.

Mr. Vaught reports that Ms. Beck has moved law firms and she was not allowed to take this matter with her, so he intends to file a substitution of attorney in this matter. Mr. Vaught advises that he has recently been provided with the file for this matter and accordingly, was not aware that there were Examiner's notes that required his attention. The court advises Mr. Vaught of the remaining issues. Mr. Cain asks the court to issue an Order to Show Cause for Distribution and points to the delays in this matter that have delayed distributions to the elderly heirs and would like to see the distributions made sooner than later. The court acknowledges Mr. Cain's position and asks counsel to meet and confer regarding this issue.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-03-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Cain, Esq., counsel for Petitioner American Research Bureau; Janelle Allen, Esq. counsel for David Coffing, Administrator.

At the Court's invitation, Mr. Cain addresses various concerns identified in the Examiner's Notes, noting at the outset that certain heirs [the Examiner's "A" list] have been identifed as heirs but are not represented by Petitioner American Research [hereinafter "ARB"] and that ARB has done what it can with respect to idenitfying and substantiating the status of these heirs.

With respect to the Examiner's "B" List, these heirs are represented by ARB and their status as heirs has been substantiated by secondary evidence (obituaries, etc.) which should be sufficient. With respect to the validity of the assignments, Mr. Cain refers the Court to Probate Code Sec. 11604.5(c) and various case authority validating or at least supportive of contingency agreements in Probate cases. The court refers Mr. Cain to Probate Code Sec. 11604.5(d), noting that the time between the signing of the assigments and the filing of the same is beyond the time-frame contemplated by that Probate Code section. Mr. Cain offers explanation for the same. Upon query by the Court, Ms. Allen offers no objection to the substance of the colloquy thus far.

After further discussion,the Court continues the matter to September 16, 2020 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody; Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not
Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janelle Allen appeared by telephone for David Coffing (Petitioner).

The Court noted that nobody else is here, that the matter had been continued for a showing of diligence in locating those entitled to notice, that there are questions regarding the Heirs that were identified by American Research Bureau and that it is not clear if they were given notice. Ms. Allen stated that they provided notice of the supplement, that they received a waiver from Lisa Castano and that the waiver was filed yesterday. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $410,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for March 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Continued To Aug-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Continued To Jul-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janelle Allen for David Coffing.

The Court stated the issue is whether there was sufficient due diligence in tracking down heirs and obtaining information on the existing heirs, that the May 22nd declaration only told the Court that they were not able to identify all of the heirs, but it did not tell what other information was found. Ms. Allen spoke of the maternal heirs and stated that the hope is that once her Client is appointed they will obtain an heir search service and file a petition to determine heirship. Ms. Allen also stated that another heirship company has reached out to them recently. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a supplement that provides additional information and includes efforts made to date. Said supplement is to filed by July 23, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
